Feijoa (Acca sellowiana) is a subtropical shrub native to South America, characterized by its edible fruit and attractive flowers. The leaves and fruit of Feijoa have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Feijoa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
